chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Crl. Revision Application No.S-100 and 102 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 05.11.2025 None is present for the applicant Mr.Muhammad Asad Shahani, Advocate for complainant Mr.Irfan Ali Talpur, D.P.G ******** It appears from the perusal of the Orders, passed by this Court, that the applicant Talib Leghari son of Ghulam Muhammad @ Haji, after obtaining bail from this Court, absconded and is presently residing either in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia or the United Arab Emirates. Although his father had appeared before this Court (he is also surety) on 08.10.2025 and undertook to produce him, no such compliance has been made. As per the Order dated 18.02.2025, NADRA was directed to immediately suspend the CNIC of the accused. In accordance with the report submitted by the Assistant Director, NADRA, available on record, the CNIC of the accused has since been digitally impounded. Furthermore, vide Order dated 27.08.2025, the office was directed to issue non-bailable warrants (NBWs) against the applicant through the Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan. As per the record, the said directions have been duly complied with and the matter has been forwarded to the Federal Secretary, Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan at Islamabad for further action. It further appears that vide Order dated 21.05.2024, the bail granted to the applicant was also recalled. In view of the foregoing circumstances, let perpetual Non-Bailable Warrants be issued against the accused/applicant. As applicant-accused father who is also surety has failed to secure his son’s attendance, the surety stands forfeited.

In view of the foregoing circumstances, let perpetual Non-Bailable Warrants be issued against the accused/applicant. As applicant-accused father who is also surety has failed to secure his son’s attendance, the surety stands forfeited. As and when the applicant-accused is arrested, he may move an appropriate application for restoration of his Revision application which will be heard and decided on its own merits accordance with law. Given the above, both these Criminal Revision Applications stand dismissed in above terms. JUDGE AHSAN K. ABRO
